revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=大毛神社| 所在地=愛知県一宮市大毛五百入塚73| 位置=| 祭神=[[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]]| 社格=式内社・旧...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=大毛神社| 所在地=愛知県一宮市大毛五百入塚73| 位置=| 祭神=[[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]]| 社格=式内社・旧郷社| 創建=不明| }}
'''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は郷社。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「大毛神社」である。
== 概況 == *[[899年]]（昌泰2年）創建。 *江戸時代には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。 *大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、[[景行天皇]]の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの[[人形]]が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。 *[[1872年]]（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる *[[1925年]]（大正14年）、郷社になる。
== 祭神 == *[[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]]
祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。
== 所在地 == *愛知県一宮市大毛五百入塚73
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分 **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「山郷西」「[[ツインアーチ138|138タワーパーク]]」行き
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおけしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:一宮市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=大毛神社| 所在地=愛知県一宮市大毛五百入塚73| 位置=| 祭神=[[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]]| 社格=式内社・旧郷社| 創建=[[899年]]（[[昌泰]]2年）| }}

'''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[氏子調#行政単位と郷社|郷社]]。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「大毛神社」である。
== 概況 == *[[899年]]（[[昌泰]]2年）創建。 *[[江戸時代]]には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。 *大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、[[景行天皇]]の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの[[人形]]が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。 *[[1872年]]（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる *[[1925年]]（大正14年）、郷社になる。
== 祭神 == *[[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]]
祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

revision.comment: 16 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 大毛神社 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[大毛]]五百入塚73 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = [[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[郷社]] | 創建 = [[899年]]（[[昌泰]]2年） | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「大毛神社」である。
== 概況 == *[[899年]]（[[昌泰]]2年）創建。 *[[江戸時代]]には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。 *大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、[[景行天皇]]の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの[[人形]]が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。 *[[1872年]]（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる *[[1925年]]（大正14年）、郷社になる。
== 祭神 == *[[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]]
祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分 **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「山郷西」「[[ツインアーチ138|138タワーパーク]]」行き
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]

{{translated page|ja|大毛神社|version=107182097|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 大毛神社 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[大毛]]五百入塚73 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = [[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[郷社]] | 創建 = [[899年]]（[[昌泰]]2年） | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「大毛神社」である。
== 概況 == *[[899年]]（[[昌泰]]2年）創建。 *[[江戸時代]]には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。 *大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、[[景行天皇]]の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの[[人形]]が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。 *[[1872年]]（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる *[[1925年]]（大正14年）、郷社になる。
== 祭神 == *[[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]]
祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分 **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「山郷西」「[[ツインアーチ138|138タワーパーク]]」行き
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]
==End Japanese==

{{translated page|ja|大毛神社|version=107182097|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 大毛神社 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[大毛]]五百入塚73 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = [[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[郷社]] | 創建 = [[899年]]（[[昌泰]]2年） | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「大毛神社」である。
== 概況 == *[[899年]]（[[昌泰]]2年）創建。 *[[江戸時代]]には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。 *大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、[[景行天皇]]の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの[[人形]]が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。 *[[1872年]]（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる *[[1925年]]（大正14年）、郷社になる。
== 祭神 == *[[オオゲツヒメ|大御食都姫命]]
祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分 **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「山郷西」「[[ツインアーチ138|138タワーパーク]]」行き
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = 大毛神社
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大毛|Q127442728}}五百入塚73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社}}。

{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「大毛神社」である。

== 概況 ==
*{{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）創建。
*{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。
*大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの{{jalink|人形|Q168658}}が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。
*{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる
*{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}（大正14年）、郷社になる。

== 祭神 ==
*{{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}

祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。

== 交通機関 ==
*{{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分
**{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「山郷西」「{{jalink|ツインアーチ138|Q11318660|138タワーパーク}}」行き

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

[[JA:大毛神社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = 大毛神社
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大毛|Q127442728}}五百入塚73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「大毛神社」である。

=== 概況 ===
*{{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）創建。
*{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。
*大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの{{jalink|人形|Q168658}}が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。
*{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる
*{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}（大正14年）、郷社になる。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}

祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。

=== 交通機関 ===
*{{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分
**{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「山郷西」「{{jalink|ツインアーチ138|Q11318660|138タワーパーク}}」行き

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

[[JA:大毛神社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = 大毛神社
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大毛|Q127442728}}五百入塚73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「大毛神社」である。

=== 概況 ===
*{{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）創建。
*{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。
*大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの{{jalink|人形|Q168658}}が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。
*{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる
*{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}（大正14年）、郷社になる。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}

祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。

=== 交通機関 ===
*{{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分
**{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「山郷西」「{{jalink|ツインアーチ138|Q11318660|138タワーパーク}}」行き

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

[[JA:大毛神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11436767}}

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = 大毛神社
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大毛|Q127442728}}五百入塚73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「大毛神社」である。

=== 概況 ===
*{{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）創建。
*{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。
*大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの{{jalink|人形|Q168658}}が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。
*{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる
*{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}（大正14年）、郷社になる。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}

祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。

=== 交通機関 ===
*{{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分
**{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「山郷西」「{{jalink|ツインアーチ138|Q11318660|138タワーパーク}}」行き

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

[[JA:大毛神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11436767}}

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = 大毛神社
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大毛|Q127442728}}五百入塚73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「大毛神社」である。

=== 概況 ===
*{{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）創建。
*{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。
*大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの{{jalink|人形|Q168658}}が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。
*{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる
*{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}（大正14年）、郷社になる。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}

祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。

=== 交通機関 ===
*{{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分
**{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「山郷西」「{{jalink|ツインアーチ138|Q11318660|138タワーパーク}}」行き

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

[[JA:大毛神社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = 大毛神社
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大毛|Q127442728}}五百入塚73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「大毛神社」である。

=== 概況 ===
*{{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）創建。
*{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。
*大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの{{jalink|人形|Q168658}}が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。
*{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる
*{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}（大正14年）、郷社になる。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}

祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。

=== 交通機関 ===
*{{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分
**{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「山郷西」「{{jalink|ツインアーチ138|Q11318660|138タワーパーク}}」行き

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

[[JA:大毛神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11436767}}

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = 大毛神社
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大毛|Q127442728}}五百入塚73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大毛神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''大毛神社'''（おおけじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「大毛神社」である。

=== 概況 ===
*{{jalink|899年|Q23734}}（{{jalink|昌泰|Q1324780}}2年）創建。
*{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には、「六社明神」、「六所明神」と称していた。
*大毛神社の別名として「ひながたの宮」と称されていたという。これは、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の皇女'''五百木入姫命'''（五百城入姫皇女）ゆかりの{{jalink|人形|Q168658}}が祀られていたからという。ここの地名が五百入塚ということ、大毛神社の古跡として、庵入塚、天子塚、景行天皇墓が挙げられている（葉栗史誌）ことから、景行天皇、五百木入姫命に関わりが深いと言われている。
*{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）、元の大毛神社に改称し、村社になる
*{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}（大正14年）、郷社になる。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|オオゲツヒメ|Q3261482|大御食都姫命}}

祭神ではないが、五百木入姫命も信仰されている。

=== 交通機関 ===
*{{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「大毛」バス停下車、徒歩5分
**{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}}{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}{{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「山郷西」「{{jalink|ツインアーチ138|Q11318660|138タワーパーク}}」行き

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Ōke|Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{jalink|1925|Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}
**From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} / {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{jalink|Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|138 Tower Park}}"

'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{jalink|1925|Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}
**From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} / {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{jalink|Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|138 Tower Park}}"

'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{jalink|1925|Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Ōke|Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{jalink|1925|Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}
**From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} / {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{jalink|Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|138 Tower Park}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Ōke|Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{jalink|1925|Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}
**From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} / {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{jalink|Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|138 Tower Park}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Ōke|Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{jalink|1925|Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}
**From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} / {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{jalink|Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|138 Tower Park}}"

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Ōke|Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{jalink|1925|Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}
**From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} / {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{jalink|Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|138 Tower Park}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Ōke|Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{jalink|899|Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{jalink|1925|Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}}
**From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} / {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{jalink|Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|138 Tower Park}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park}}"

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park}}"

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park}}"

{{nihongo|'''Ōke Shrine'''|大毛神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=26 October 2014 (Sun) 04:27 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称 = Ōke Shrine
 | 画像 = [[File:Ohke-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
 | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Ōke|Q127442728|WD=Q127442728|1=Ōke}} Iori-zuka 73
 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}
 | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
 | 創建 = {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2)
 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōke Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ōke Shrine''' (おおけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

It is the "Ōke Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

=== Overview ===
*Founded in {{ill|lt=899|Q23734|WD=Q23734|1=899}} (Shōtai 2).
*In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Rokusha Myōjin" and "Rokusho Myōjin."
*It was also called "Hinagata no Miya." This is said to be because a doll associated with Imperial Princess Iori-iri-hime (daughter of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}) was enshrined here. Since the local place name is Iori-zuka and old sites linked with Ōke Shrine include An-iri-zuka, Tenshi-zuka, and Emperor Keikō's tomb (Haguri history records), it is said to have deep ties to Emperor Keikō and Princess Iori-iri-hime.
*In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it reverted to the old name Ōke Shrine and became a village shrine.
*In {{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|WD=Q18107|1=1925}} (Taishō 14), it became a gōsha.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}}

Though not an official enshrined deity, Princess Iori-iri-hime is also worshipped.

=== Transportation ===
*5 minutes on foot from "Ōke" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}
**From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (platform 3 at Meitetsu Ichinomiya bus terminal), bound for "Yamasato Nishi" or "{{ill|lt=Twin Arch 138|Q11318660|WD=Q11318660|lt=138 Tower Park|1=Twin Arch 138}}"
